evals.prompt:
I just ran astrodb-match-schema on my parallax catalog and here's the mapping I got. Can you validate this against the actual data file to make sure it's safe to ingest?

Data file: /Users/kelle/Hunter College Dropbox/Kelle Cruz/SIMPLE/astrodb-bot/.claude/skills/astrodb-validate-schema-mapping/evals/data/parallax_catalog_with_nulls.ecsv

Schema file: /Users/kelle/Hunter College Dropbox/Kelle Cruz/SIMPLE/astrotemplate-db/schema.yaml

| Input Column | Description | Units | Type | DB Table | DB Field | Confidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| source_id | Primary identifier | — | str | Sources | source | High | Primary key |
| ra | Right ascension | deg | float64 | Sources | ra_deg | High | |
| dec | Declination | deg | float64 | Sources | dec_deg | High | |
| plx | Parallax | mas | float64 | Parallaxes | parallax_mas | High | |
| e_plx | Parallax uncertainty | mas | float64 | Parallaxes | parallax_error | High | |
| ref | Reference bibcode | — | str | Publications | reference | High | |
evals.expected_output: A validation report identifying that the 'plx' column (mapped to Parallaxes.parallax_mas) has 3 null values out of 8 rows. The schema should show that parallax_mas is nullable (or warn about null values). Report should also confirm clean types for all columns and produce a summary.

assertions.text: The report identifies that the 'plx' column has 3 null/missing values (3 out of 8 rows)
The 'e_plx' column is reported as having no null values
The report checks the schema's nullable setting for Parallaxes.parallax_mas and reports it
The float64 type of plx and e_plx columns is confirmed compatible with the schema's double/float type
A validation report file is written to astrodb-build-artifacts/schema-validation-report.md and the path is given to the user
The output includes a clear summary section counting the number of nullable violations and type mismatches found

evals.prompt:
Here's my schema mapping for a photometry catalog. Please check it against the data — I'm especially worried about the magnitude column since some entries might not be numbers.

Data file: /Users/kelle/Hunter College Dropbox/Kelle Cruz/SIMPLE/astrodb-bot/.claude/skills/astrodb-validate-schema-mapping/evals/data/photometry_type_mismatch.ecsv

Schema: /Users/kelle/Hunter College Dropbox/Kelle Cruz/SIMPLE/astrotemplate-db/schema.yaml

| Input Column | Description | Units | Type | DB Table | DB Field | Confidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| name | Source designation | — | str | Sources | source | High | Primary key |
| band | Filter name | — | str | PhotometryFilters | band | High | Needs SVO ID |
| mag | Magnitude | mag | str | Photometry | magnitude | High | |
| mag_err | Magnitude uncertainty | mag | float64 | Photometry | magnitude_error | High | |
| telescope | Telescope name | — | str | Telescopes | telescope | High | |
| ref | Reference | — | str | Publications | reference | High | |
evals.expected_output: A validation report flagging that 'mag' column is type string but Photometry.magnitude expects a double/float — this is a type mismatch. The report should also note that values like 'sat' and 'nd' in the mag column are the cause. No nullable violations expected.

assertions.text: The report flags that the 'mag' column (data type string/object) is incompatible with Photometry.magnitude which expects a numeric (double/float) type
The 'mag_err' column is reported as type-compatible with magnitude_error (both float)
No nullable violations are reported (no null values found in non-nullable fields in this dataset)
The report includes a recommendation about how to handle the type mismatch — e.g., filter out non-numeric values or cast the column
The summary correctly shows 1 type mismatch and 0 nullable violations

evals.prompt:
I mapped my radial velocity catalog to AstroDB and want to double-check everything looks good before I write the ingestion script. Here's the mapping:

Data: /Users/kelle/Hunter College Dropbox/Kelle Cruz/SIMPLE/astrodb-bot/.claude/skills/astrodb-validate-schema-mapping/evals/data/clean_rv_catalog.ecsv

Schema: /Users/kelle/Hunter College Dropbox/Kelle Cruz/SIMPLE/astrotemplate-db/schema.yaml

| Input Column | Description | Units | Type | DB Table | DB Field | Confidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| source | Source identifier | — | str | Sources | source | High | Foreign key to Sources |
| rv_kms | Radial velocity | km/s | float64 | RadialVelocities | rv_kms | High | |
| rv_error | RV uncertainty | km/s | float64 | RadialVelocities | rv_error | High | |
| reference | Reference bibcode | — | str | Publications | reference | High | |
evals.expected_output: A clean validation report with no issues — all columns have correct types and no null values. Report should confirm all 4 mappings passed and give the user a green light to proceed with ingestion.

assertions.text: The report confirms no nullable violations and no type mismatches
The report shows that all 4 mapped columns passed validation
The 'rv_kms' column (float64) is confirmed compatible with RadialVelocities.rv_kms schema type
The summary clearly states the mapping is clean and it is safe to proceed with ingestion
A report file is written to astrodb-build-artifacts/schema-validation-report.md and the path is given to the user
